content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 7,
1992
Rusty Souza
15632
W.
E Street
Kerman,
CA
93630
Re:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
1—92—615
Dear Mr.
Souza:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
concerning potential conflict—of—interest situations which may
arise under the Political Reform Act (the “Act”)
1 regarding your
duties as an elected official of a special fire district board.
Since your advice request seeks general guidance,
we are treating
your request as one for informal assistance.
2
This letter confirms advice provided to you on
November 6,
1992.
Please note that our advice is limited to
provisions of the Act.
We cannot provide advice about other
conflict—of—interest laws.
Thus, it would be advisable to contact
the Attorney General’s Office with respect to the doctrine of
“incompatible offices” and other provisions of law that might
apply to your facts,
such as Government Code Section 1090.
QUESTION
Does your employment with the California Department of
Forestry and Fire Protection or your status as an elected official
of a special fire district create conflicts of interest with
respect to governmental decisions which may arise regarding an
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et .
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3) .)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
1-92-615
Page
2
existing contract between the two agencies,
or other governmental
decisions?
CONCLUSION
Neither your salary from nor employment with the California
Department of Forestry and Fire Protection will create an economic
interest in the special fire district which could result in a
conflict of interest.
Furthermore, your disqualification is not
required and you may participate in governmental decisions
regarding the state agency contract,
and other governmental
decisions,
as long as the decisions will not have a material
financial effect on your economic interests.
FACTS
You are employed with the California Department of Forestry
and Fire Protection,
a state agency.
You have recently been
elected to a local special fire protection district,
a local
government agency,
which has an existing contract with your state
agency employer.
DISCUSSION
The Act does not prohibit a public official from holding
multiple public positions, either within a single agency or
different agencies.
(Bluett Advice Letter,
No.
1-92-287.)
However,
as discussed in our telephone conversation of
November 6,
1992,
the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act
(Section 87100
g., discussed below)
prohibit a public
official from participating in a decision which will have a
financial effect on his or her economic interests.
However, this
prohibition is applied on a decision-by-decision basis.
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making,
or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest.
Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a
financial interest if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from the effect on the public generally,
on the official or a
member of his or her immediate family
3 or on:
(c)
Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
An official’s “immediate family” includes his spouse and
dependent children.
(Section 82029.)


Our File No.
1-92—615
Page
3
official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
Section 87103(c).
You indicated in our telephone conversation that the special
fire district board to which you have been elected has a contract
with your employing agency.
As an elected official of the special
fire district,
you are concerned about potential conflicts of
interest which may arise if you participate in governmental
decisions relating to the contract between the two agencies.
Section 82030 defines the term “income” for the purposes of
the Act.
Subdivision
(a)
of Section 82030 includes in this
definition payments received for wages, salary,
payment of
indebtedness and reimbursement for expenses.
Accordingly,
payments received under contract by a public official may be
sources of income to the official.
(Section 87103(c).)
You have not provided us with information as to whether you
receive compensation for your services as a member of the board of
the special fire district or compensation as part of the special
district’s contract with your state agency employer.
However,
salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem received from a
state,
local,
or federal government agency is not regarded as
“income” for purposes of the Act.
(Section 82030(b) (2).)
According to your facts, your employer is a state agency and
the special district is a government agency under the Act.
(Sections 82041 and 82049.)
Thus,
any income received from these
agencies would not be a disqualifying economic interest to you
pursuant to the provisions of Section 82030(b) (2).
Absent some other disqualifying financial interest as set
forth in Section 87103,
or some direct financial effect on you
personally resulting from a governmental decision,
you will not
have conflicts of interest under the Act due to your position wit!
the State of California or your elective office with the local
government agency because of the contract relationship between th
two governmental entities.
Thus, it appears that you may
participate in governmental decisions regarding the state agency
contract which may arise in your elective capacity and other
governmental decisions.


Our File No.
1—92-615
Page
4
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322—59O1.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabriri
Acting General Counsel
‘ief)
By:
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel,
Legal Division
LM:docs:92615
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 1O! per page.
